title: NOC 92012 – Supervisors, food and beverage processing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92012 are the operational leaders of Canada’s multi-billion dollar food and beverage manufacturing sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals manage the production lines for everything from dairy and meat processing to brewery and bakery operations. In 2026, as Canada prioritizes global food security and "Value-Added" agricultural exports, these supervisors are at the absolute forefront of the industry’s transition toward fully automated, high-precision processing. For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the Agri-Food Immigration Pilot (Transition Stream) and Express Entry Category-Based Selection.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9201 – Supervisors, processing and manufacturing occupationsNOC Code92012

duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers who process, package, and grade food and beverage products.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate activities with other departments to ensure seamless production flow.
Resolve work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product quality.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage equipment maintenance schedules to prevent downtime.
Ensure strict adherence to HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) and SQF (Safe Quality Food) standards.
Train workers in job duties, sanitation procedures, and occupational health and safety (OHS) policies.
Prepare production and safety reports for senior plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific processing or manufacturing occupation supervised are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma or university degree in food science, microbiology, or a related field is increasingly preferred for modern automated plants.
Certifications:Food Safety Certification (Advanced level) is mandatory.HACCP Certification is often a prerequisite for supervisory roles.WHMIS and First Aid certifications are standard requirements.
Food Safety Certification (Advanced level) is mandatory.
HACCP Certification is often a prerequisite for supervisory roles.
WHMIS and First Aid certifications are standard requirements.
Strong leadership, problem-solving skills, and proficiency in Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Total annual earnings for experienced supervisors in 2026 often exceed $85,000 - $110,000, especially when including overtime and performance bonuses in high-volume export plants.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the food processing sector will see a 14% growth in supervisory positions by 2033. As Canada invests in "Plant-Based Protein" hubs and specialized beverage manufacturing (e.g., craft spirits and functional drinks), the need for supervisors who understand both traditional processing and new food technologies will continue to rise.
labour_market_demand: The demand for food and beverage processing supervisors is Very Good, particularly in provinces with massive agricultural footprints. In 2026, Ontario, Quebec, and Saskatchewan are reporting significant labor shortages in senior production roles. According to Food Processing Skills Canada (FPSC), the industry requires thousands of new supervisors to manage the integration of AI-driven quality control and advanced packaging systems.
Global Food Hub: Work in a country that is a world-leading exporter of safe, high-quality food products.
Technological Innovation: Manage "Smart Factories" utilizing robotic arms for sorting, AI for predictive maintenance, and blockchain for food traceability.
Stable Industry: The food and beverage sector is recession-proof, providing long-term job security and steady year-round employment.
Strategic PR Pathways: Highly valued in the Express Entry category-based draws for Agriculture and specific Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in the "Agriculture and Agri-Food" category.
Agri-Food Immigration Pilot (Transition Stream): A specialized route for supervisors with experience in meat, greenhouse, or mushroom production.
Saskatchewan INP (Occupations In-Demand): Frequently prioritizes supervisors in the food processing sector.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based food or beverage company.
